---
title: "Lesson - Update README"
id: lesson_update_readme
author: brad_cannell
date: yyyy-mm-dd
date-modified: yyyy-mm-dd
audience: course_authors
category: lesson_development
tags:
  - r
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  eval: false
---

# Overview

- Conversation about updating the README.

# Prerequisites
- **Git Pull** to make sure you have the most current version of the prompts.
- Install in the system kernel. The virtual environments cause too many problems with Box.
- `import sys`
- `print(sys.executable)` Path the Python interpreter that is actually running the code. 
- Terminal: `which pip` Pip that will be used to install Python packages
- Terminal: `/usr/local/bin/python3 -m pip install keyring openai anthropic jupyter -U chatlas shiny`

# Load Packages

```{python}
from chatlas import ChatAnthropic
from openai import OpenAI
from httpcore import stream
from datetime import date
from pathlib import Path
import chatlas as ctl
import anthropic
import keyring
import json
import os
```

# File Paths and Environment Variables

They are passed to the AI assistant below.

```{python}
# OpenAI Models: https://platform.openai.com/docs/models
# Anthropic Models: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/overview
model = "gpt-5-nano-2025-08-07"

# Today's date
today = date.today().isoformat()

# Grab the course path and name
course_path = Path(
    "path"
)
course_name = course_path.name

# Enter lesson paths and names
lesson_specs = [
    (
        "folder",
        "file",
    ),
    (
        "folder",
        "file",
    ),
    (
        "folder",
        "file",
    ),
]

# Grab the content for each lesson
lessons_content_dict = {}
for *folders, lesson_name in lesson_specs:
    lesson_path = course_path.joinpath(*folders, f"{lesson_name}.qmd")
    if not lesson_path.exists():
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Missing lesson file: {lesson_path}")
    lessons_content_dict[lesson_name] = lesson_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

# Convert the lessons_content_dict dict to a string
lessons_content_string = "\n\n".join(
    f"# {lesson_name}\n\n{content}"
    for lesson_name, content in lessons_content_dict.items()
)

# Create a bulleted list of lesson names
lesson_names_string = "\n".join(
    f"- {lesson_name}"
    for *_, lesson_name in lesson_specs
)

# Prompt paths
path_template_prompt_readme = '/Users/bradcannell/Desktop/Git/ewb-ai-assistant/prompts/Courses/Prompt - README.md'
```

# Load helper functions

Run in terminal:
- `/usr/local/bin/python3 -m pip install --editable "/Users/bradcannell/Desktop/Git/AI/my-ai-assistant/py_scripts"`
- Restart interpreter.

```{python}
# Load functions from py_scripts/py_scripts
from py_scripts import (
    prompt_extract_system_prompt,
    prompt_extract_user_message,
    prompt_fill_placeholders,
)
```

# Expose API

This assumes you've already created an API key on [openai's website](https://platform.openai.com/api-keys), and that you saved the API key in keyring using `keyring.set_password("API Name", "User", "API Key")`.

```{python}
# Retrieve the password from the system keyring
openai_api = keyring.get_password("ewb-course-development", "brad")

if openai_api is not None:
    # Set the retrieved value as a temporary environment variable
    os.environ['OPENAI_API_KEY'] = openai_api
    print("Environment variable 'OPENAI_API_KEY' set successfully within the script.")
else:
    print("Could not retrieve password from keyring.")
```

# Read the Template Prompt

```{python}
# Raw system (developer) section of the prompt template.
system_prompt = prompt_extract_system_prompt(path_template_prompt_readme)

# Raw user message section of the prompt template.
user_message_raw = prompt_extract_user_message(path_template_prompt_readme)

# Inject course and lesson name into user message
user_message = prompt_fill_placeholders(
    text = user_message_raw,
    replacements = {
        "course_name": course_name,
        "lesson_names": lesson_names_string,
        "lesson_content": lessons_content_string
    },
)
```

# Create Chat Object

```{python}
chat = ctl.ChatOpenAI(
    model = model,
    system_prompt = system_prompt
)
```

# Pass the Template Prompt to the AI Assistant

```{python}
chat.chat(user_message, echo='none')
```

## Response

```{python}
#| echo: false
print(chat)
```

# Follow-up and Refinement

A section for optional follow-up questions and refinements. 
- Optionally, create a fork of the chat. `import copy; chat_fork = copy.deepcopy(chat)`
- Asks follow-up questions in interactive mode - `chat.app()`.

# Save Chat History

Optional, but necessary to return to the conversation later.

```{python}
# Export chat history as a markdown file
# This version is easier to pass back to to the chat object in the future.
chat_history_path_md = (course_path / "ai" / ("readme_" + today + ".md"))
chat.export(chat_history_path_md, overwrite=True)

# Export chat history as an HTML file
chat_history_path_html = (course_path / "ai" / ("readme_" + today + ".html"))
chat.export(chat_history_path_html, overwrite=True)

# Export chat history as a json file
# Useful for saving/loading the history to/from a database or file.
# chat_history_path_json = (lesson_folder_path / "ai" / ("lesson_spelling_grammar_" + today + ".json"))
# with open(chat_history_path_json, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
#     json.dump(
#         [json.loads(x) for x in turns_json],
#         f,
#         indent=2,
#         ensure_ascii=False
#     )
```

## Condense

You can also optionally condense the history by asking the LLM to summarize it. This is useful if you want to keep the context of the conversation, but don’t want to provide the entire history to the model on each new submission.

```{python}
# chat.chat("Can you summarize our conversation so far?")
# chat.set_turns([chat.get_last_turn()])
```

## Load chat history

To optionally load the chat history later.

```{python}
# Import chat history as a md file
# chat_history_path_md = (lesson_folder_path / "ai" / ("lesson_revisions_history_" + "yyyy-mm-dd" + ".md"))
# history_md = chat_history_path_md.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

# Import chat history as a json file
# chat_history_path_json = (lesson_folder_path / "ai" / ("lesson_revisions_history_" + "yyyy-mm-dd" + ".json"))
# history_json = json.loads(chat_history_path_json.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
```

# Render to HTML

Render to HTML (Optional)
- Click the Preview button.
- Type `quarto render "path/Lesson Revisions.qmd"` in the terminal.
